title: Handling Punctuation & Numbers
concept: Deciding whether to keep or strip numbers and punctuation depends on the task; in sentiment analysis, exclamation marks add value, while in general classification, they add noise.

exercises.prompt: When cleaning text for an AI, what should you do with punctuation marks like exclamation points?

options.text: Keep them if they add emotional value

exercises.explain: Sometimes symbols like "!" or "?" carry emotional meaning (sentiment) that an AI needs to understand the text's mood.

cards.title: Clutter or Clues?
cards.body: Should we clean up text by throwing away every single comma, period, and number? Not so fast! Sometimes, a simple "!" can completely change how an AI understands a sentence.

cards.title: Custom Cleaning
cards.body: Punctuation and numbers can either be helpful clues or useless clutter. In NLP, deciding what to keep or throw away depends entirely on what you want your AI model to achieve.

cards.title: The Party Decorator
cards.body: Think of cleaning a house. If you are decorating for a party, you want to keep the bright balloons (exclamation marks) because they show excitement! But if you are just counting the furniture, those balloons are just distracting clutter that you should throw away.

cards.title: Context is King
cards.body: If your AI is predicting movie reviews, "Best movie ever!!!" has a different emotional weight than "Best movie ever." Keeping the exclamation marks helps detect that extra excitement. But if the AI is just grouping news articles by topic, those exclamation marks don't help and should be stripped.

cards.title: The Clean-All Trap
cards.body: Many beginners think you must ALWAYS strip all punctuation and numbers to get clean text. In reality, removing them can sometimes destroy key meaning, especially when detecting sarcasm, excitement, or specific quantities like dates and prices.

cards.title: Keep vs. Strip
cards.body: See how the value of punctuation changes depending on what we want the AI to do.

left.text: Keep "!!!" because it shows strong positive emotion.

right.text: Remove "!!!" because they don't help identify the topic.

exercises.prompt: For which task would keeping exclamation marks (!) be MOST useful for the AI?

options.text: Counting the total number of words

exercises.explain: Sentiment analysis is all about detecting feelings, and exclamation marks are strong indicators of high excitement or anger.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you are building an AI to extract product prices from web pages, you should strip out all numbers.
exercises.explain: Numbers carry vital context for things like dates, prices, and measurements. Stripping them would lose this key information.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the rule about general classification:
exercises.sentence: In general classification tasks, punctuation is often removed because it adds noise.
exercises.explain: For general topic sorting, extra punctuation like question marks and exclamation points doesn't help group the text and is treated as useless noise.

exercises.prompt: Two AIs process the sentence: "Love this!" for a review rating task. Which one uses the best strategy?

options.text: AI Model A: It simplifies the text to 'love this' to save processing time.
AI Model B: It keeps the '!' because sentiment analysis benefits from emotional cues.
AI Model C: It replaces the exclamation mark with a neutral period.

exercises.explain: AI Model B keeps the exclamation mark, which acts as a strong signal of positive excitement (sentiment). Model A stripped it, losing that emotional hint.

exercises.prompt: You are building an AI helper to detect if customer reviews are angry or happy. Which punctuation strategy makes the most sense?

options.text: Strip all numbers and symbols to save computer memory
Keep exclamation marks to help detect strong emotions
Replace all punctuation marks with random numbers
Remove everything except lowercase letters to make it simple

exercises.explain: Exactly! Keeping punctuation like exclamation points or question marks helps a sentiment model understand the emotional energy behind the text.
